nodes.description: Integrates all light qualities that activate photoreceptors; WT default = 1.0 (light-grown).
Warm-vs-cool ambient temperature cue; high Temperature inhibits phyB and ELF3, and stabilizes PIF4.
Red-light photoreceptor (Pr/Pfr); dominant repressor of elongation. Active Pfr degrades PIFs and inhibits COP1. Thermally reverts to inactive Pr at warm temperature.
Far-red photoreceptor; mediates VLFR and far-red HIR; inhibits COP1 through direct binding.
Blue-light photoreceptor; inhibits COP1 and destabilizes PIF4/PIF7.
Blue-light photoreceptor (redundant with CRY1); inhibits COP1.
UV-B photoreceptor; monomerizes on UV-B and binds COP1, stabilizing HY5 and directly destabilizing PIFs.
RING E3 ligase; multi-output scaffold targeting HY5, HFR1, BBX21, HYH for degradation in the dark. Activity requires SPA1; inhibited by all five photoreceptors.
COP1 co-activator in the CUL4-COP1-SPA1 complex; constitutively expressed (no curated upstream).
CUL4-DDB1-DET1 complex component; stabilizes PIFs in darkness. Residual gap: COP10/DDB1 upstream not curated; treated as source.
bZIP master photomorphogenic TF; stabilized by light (COP1 inhibition) and UV-B; represses elongation genes. Destabilized at warm temperature.
bHLH antagonist of PIFs; heterodimerizes with PIF4/PIF5/PIF7 to block DNA binding. Degraded by COP1.
HY5 partner; stabilizes HY5 binding at photomorphogenic promoters. Degraded by COP1.
bHLH elongation TF; stabilized by DET1 in darkness; phosphorylated/degraded by phyB in light; DELLA and BIN2 sequester/inhibit it.
bHLH master integrator of thermomorphogenesis, shade, and BR/auxin crosstalk; direct activator of YUC8, TAA1, SAUR19, ARF6.
bHLH elongation TF; redundant with PIF4 in shade response.
bHLH primary shade responder; dephosphorylation on low R:FR releases it into the nucleus to induce auxin biosynthesis (YUC8).
Trimeric clock complex that represses PIF4/PIF5 transcription at dusk; destabilized at warm temperature.
Steroid 5α-reductase in BR biosynthesis; constitutive (residual gap: no curated upstream).
BR biosynthesis rate-limiting enzyme; auto-regulated by BZR1 negative feedback.
BR-26-hydroxylase; inactivates BRs; induced by phyB in light.
BR-catabolizing cytochrome P450; constitutively expressed (residual gap).
Growth-promoting hormone. ONLY outgoing edge = BR -> BRI1 (Perception Gate enforcement).
BR receptor leucine-rich-repeat kinase at the plasma membrane; partners with BAK1 to inhibit BIN2.
SERK3 co-receptor of BRI1; constitutively expressed (source — BR-signaling competent independent of PIF4 in the minimal model to avoid positive-feedback with BIN2).
GSK3-like kinase; phosphorylates BZR1 to exclude it from the nucleus; inhibited by BRI1/BAK1 signaling and by SPA1/COP1 in dark.
BR-responsive TF; unphosphorylated form enters nucleus; activates elongation genes and represses DWF4 (negative auto-feedback).
Dephosphorylates BZR1 (activating); constitutively expressed.
GA biosynthesis enzyme (oxidation step); constitutive source (residual gap: Light repression caught via Light -> GA4 not modeled here).
GA biosynthesis last step; repressed by ABA.
GA catabolism (deactivates bioactive GAs); induced by ABA.
Bioactive GA; product of GA20ox/GA3ox; inactivated by GA2ox. ONLY outgoing edge = GA4 -> GID1 (Perception Gate).
GA soluble receptor; composite of GID1A, GID1B, GID1C (triple redundant). GA-bound GID1 recruits DELLA to SCF^SLY1 for degradation.
Quintuple-redundant GA repressor family; directly sequesters/inhibits PIF3, PIF4, ARF6, and BZR1. Induced by ABA; degraded in a GA/GID1-dependent manner.
Rate-limiting enzyme in IAA biosynthesis (IPA pathway); induced by PIF4.
Flavin monooxygenase catalyzing final step of IAA biosynthesis; PIF4/PIF7 targets. Composite stand-in for YUC1/2/5/8/9.
Primary auxin; promotes cell elongation. ONLY outgoing edge = Auxin -> TIR1 (Perception Gate).
F-box auxin receptor; composite for TIR1, AFB1, AFB2, AFB5. Auxin-bound TIR1 ubiquitinates Aux/IAA for degradation.
Short-lived repressors of ARF transcription; degraded in response to auxin via TIR1.
Class-A ARF TFs; positive regulators of cell elongation; composite for ARF6, ARF7, ARF8. Activated by auxin via AUX_IAA degradation; induced by BZR1 and PIF4; inhibited by DELLA.
Gaseous hormone; inhibits CTR1 via ETR1 receptor family. ONLY outgoing edge = Ethylene -> CTR1.
Raf-like kinase; in absence of ethylene, phosphorylates and inhibits EIN2. Ethylene binding to ETR1 releases CTR1 inhibition on EIN2.
Master positive regulator of ethylene signaling; unphosphorylated form triggers EIN3 stabilization.
Master ethylene-responsive TF; in dark, EIN3 stabilization shortens hypocotyl (triple response).
Stress hormone; antagonizes GA at biosynthesis/catabolism level and stabilizes DELLA. Treated as source (NCED biosynthesis not curated for hypocotyl context).
Length of the seedling hypocotyl; integrates BR, GA, auxin, ethylene, and light signaling. Activators promote elongation (+1); inhibitors shorten (-1).

edges.mechanism: Red-light absorption converts inactive Pr phyB to active Pfr phyB form in the nucleus.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Requires PIF Degradation and Sequestration to Induce Light Responses across a Wide Range of Light Conditions
evidence.authors: Park E, Kim Y, Choi G
evidence.evidence_sentence: phyB induces light responses across a wide range of light conditions
evidence.claim: Red light photoconverts inactive Pr to active Pfr form of phyB

edges.mechanism: Far-red absorption activates phyA; photolabile Pfr translocates to the nucleus.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Requires PIF Degradation and Sequestration to Induce Light Responses across a Wide Range of Light Conditions

evidence.claim: Far-red light photoactivates phyA

evidence.evidence_sentence: Upon absorption of photons, cryptochromes are photoexcited involving electron transfer

evidence.authors: Wang X, Wang Q, Nguyen P, Lin C
evidence.evidence_sentence: cryptochromes are photoexcited involving electron transfer
evidence.claim: Blue light photoactivates CRY2 (and triggers CRY2 turnover at high fluence)

evidence.claim: UV-B causes UVR8 dimer to monomerize and activates UV-B signaling

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature disrupts Evening Complex (ELF3/ELF4/LUX) assembly via ELF3 prion-like domain phase transition.

evidence.claim: Warm temperature inactivates ELF3 (via prion-like domain phase transition + XBAT31-mediated degradation)

edges.mechanism: Warm temperature (28 C) increases PIF4 transcript and protein stability.

evidence.title: PIF4 Integrates Multiple Environmental and Hormonal Signals for Plant Growth Regulation in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Choi H, Oh E
evidence.evidence_sentence: high temperature increases PIF4 activity
evidence.claim: Warm temperature induces PIF4 expression and activity by releasing EC repression and decreasing phyB Pfr

evidence.title: A warm temperature-released negative feedback loop fine-tunes PIF4-mediated thermomorphogenesis in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Li H, Xue M, Zhang H, Zhao F, Li X, Yu S, Jiang D
evidence.evidence_sentence: High temperature triggers the degradation of HY5
evidence.claim: Warm temperature triggers HY5 protein degradation

edges.mechanism: Active phyB physically binds COP1 and promotes its nuclear exclusion, reducing COP1 E3 activity.

evidence.title: Interaction of COP1 and UVR8 regulates UV-B-induced photomorphogenesis and stress acclimation in Arabidopsis

evidence.title: Interaction of COP1 and UVR8 regulates UV-B-induced photomorphogenesis and stress acclimation in Arabidopsis

evidence.title: Interaction of COP1 and UVR8 regulates UV-B-induced photomorphogenesis and stress acclimation in Arabidopsis

evidence.authors: Sandhu KS, Hagely K, Neff MM
evidence.evidence_sentence: BAS1 hydroxylates brassinolide and castasterone to their respective inactive C-26 hydroxy products
evidence.claim: BAS1/CYP734A1 catalyzes C-26 hydroxylation inactivating BL and CS

edges.mechanism: SOB7/CYP72C1 redundantly inactivates BRs alongside BAS1.

evidence.title: Genetic Interactions Between Brassinosteroid-Inactivating P450s and Photomorphogenic Photoreceptors in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sandhu KS, Hagely K, Neff MM
evidence.evidence_sentence: SOB7 acts on precursors of BR biosynthesis
evidence.claim: SOB7/CYP72C1 inactivates BR biosynthesis precursors

edges.mechanism: phyB-HY5 axis promotes BAS1 expression, contributing to BR catabolism in the light.

evidence.title: Genetic Interactions Between Brassinosteroid-Inactivating P450s and Photomorphogenic Photoreceptors in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Sandhu KS, Hagely K, Neff MM
evidence.evidence_sentence: BAS1-GUS expression in shoot apex was much more prominent in wild-type PHYB background than phyB-9 background in red light
evidence.claim: phyB modulates BAS1 expression in shoot apex; phyB-9 reduces BAS1 expression

edges.mechanism: BZR1 represses DWF4 transcription as negative-feedback regulation of BR biosynthesis.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: BZR1 represses BR biosynthetic gene DWF4 forming negative feedback
evidence.claim: BZR1 negatively feeds back to DWF4 transcription, maintaining BR homeostasis

edges.mechanism: Brassinolide binds the BRI1 extracellular LRR domain, activating the receptor kinase.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR directly binds to extracellular domain of BRI1 at pocket formed by island loop
evidence.claim: Brassinosteroid binds to extracellular LRR-island domain of BRI1 receptor kinase

edges.mechanism: Active BRI1 (via BSK/BSU1) dephosphorylates and inhibits BIN2.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: BSU1 phosphatase dephosphorylates and inactivates GSK3-like kinase BIN2
evidence.claim: BSU1 phosphatase dephosphorylates and inactivates GSK3 kinase BIN2

edges.mechanism: BAK1 heterodimerizes with BRI1; active BRI1/BAK1 complex inhibits BIN2 activity.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR acts as molecular glue to bring together BRI1 and BAK1; sequential transphosphorylation activates kinases
evidence.claim: BR-bound BRI1 dimerizes with BAK1 co-receptor and transphosphorylates it

edges.mechanism: BIN2 phosphorylates BZR1 to exclude it from the nucleus and target it for degradation.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: BIN2 phosphorylates BZR1 to inhibit nuclear localization and DNA-binding activity
evidence.claim: BIN2 phosphorylates BZR1 to retain it in cytoplasm and inhibit DNA binding

edges.mechanism: PP2A dephosphorylates BZR1, activating its nuclear function.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: PROTEIN PHOSPHATASE 2A dephosphorylates BZR1 when BR levels are high
evidence.claim: PP2A dephosphorylates BZR1, activating it for nuclear localization

edges.mechanism: DELLA directly binds BZR1 and inhibits BZR1-DNA binding.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins inhibit DNA binding activities of BZR1
evidence.claim: DELLA directly binds BZR1 to inhibit DNA binding

edges.mechanism: GA20ox1 catalyzes 20-oxidation of GA12 precursors toward bioactive GA4.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA20ox1 plays a more prominent role in elongation
evidence.claim: GA20ox1 catalyzes oxidation steps producing GA9 precursor

edges.mechanism: GA3ox1 catalyzes the final 3β-hydroxylation producing bioactive GA4.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA3ox1 has predominant role in plant development; ga3ox1 mutant displays semi-dwarf phenotype
evidence.claim: GA3ox1 catalyzes 3β-hydroxylation producing bioactive GA4

edges.mechanism: GA2ox1 2β-hydroxylates bioactive GAs, inactivating them.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA2ox overexpression lines show late flowering and dwarf phenotypes
evidence.claim: GA2ox catalyzes 2β-hydroxylation inactivating bioactive GAs

edges.mechanism: ABA represses GA3ox1 expression, reducing bioactive GA4.

evidence.title: Abscisic acid inhibits hypocotyl elongation acting on gibberellins, DELLA proteins and auxin

evidence.title: Abscisic acid inhibits hypocotyl elongation acting on gibberellins, DELLA proteins and auxin
evidence.authors: Lorrai R, Boccaccini A, Ruta V, Possenti M, Costantino P, Vittorioso P

evidence.claim: ABA represses GA20ox1 transcription

edges.mechanism: ABA induces GA2ox1 expression, accelerating GA inactivation.

evidence.title: Abscisic acid inhibits hypocotyl elongation acting on gibberellins, DELLA proteins and auxin
evidence.authors: Lorrai R, Boccaccini A, Ruta V, Possenti M, Costantino P, Vittorioso P

evidence.claim: ABA induces GA2ox catabolic genes to reduce active GA

edges.mechanism: ABA stabilizes DELLA proteins and promotes DELLA-mediated repression.

evidence.title: Abscisic acid inhibits hypocotyl elongation acting on gibberellins, DELLA proteins and auxin
evidence.authors: Lorrai R, Boccaccini A, Ruta V, Possenti M, Costantino P, Vittorioso P

evidence.claim: ABA stabilizes DELLA proteins (RGA, GAI) by reducing GA-induced degradation

evidence.authors: Sun TP

edges.mechanism: GA-bound GID1 targets DELLA proteins for proteasomal degradation.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA-GID1 binding induces DELLA ubiquitination by SCFSLY1
evidence.claim: GA-GID1 binds DELLA and recruits SCF(SLY1) for ubiquitination and degradation

evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins directly interact with PIF3 and PIF4, inactivating their function

evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY

edges.mechanism: CTR1 phosphorylates the EIN2 C-terminal domain, inhibiting EIN2 cleavage.

evidence.title: Seedling morphogenesis: when ethylene meets high ambient temperature
evidence.authors: Shi J, Zhu Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: active CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2 to suppress signaling
evidence.claim: CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2 C-terminus, retaining it at ER, in absence of ethylene

edges.mechanism: Uncleaved/cleaved EIN2 C-terminus stabilizes EIN3 by inhibiting EBF1/EBF2 F-box proteins.

evidence.title: Ethylene-Induced Stabilization of EIN3 and EIL1 Is Mediated by Proteasomal Degradation of EBF1 and EBF2 That Requires EIN2 in Arabidopsis

edges.mechanism: EIN3 and PIF3 cooperatively induce elongation genes in dark; EIN3 stabilizes PIF3 protein.

evidence.title: Seedling morphogenesis: when ethylene meets high ambient temperature
evidence.authors: Shi J, Zhu Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: ethylene induces the transcription of PIF3 through EIN3
evidence.claim: EIN3 activates PIF3 transcription in light, contributing to ethylene-induced apical hook

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis Phytochrome-Interacting Factor PIF7, Together with PIF3 and PIF4, Regulates Responses to Prolonged Red Light by Modulating phyB Levels

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly induces SAUR-family and expansin genes driving cell elongation.

evidence.authors: Choi H, Oh E
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 increases auxin levels by directly activating the expression of key auxin biosynthesis genes; pif4 mutant insensitive to high temperatures
evidence.claim: PIF4 directly activates auxin biosynthesis (YUC8, TAA1, CYP79B2), Aux/IAAs, and cell wall genes

edges.mechanism: PIF5 redundantly with PIF3/4 activates elongation genes.

evidence.title: Degradation of the transcription factors PIF4 and PIF5 under UV-B promotes UVR8-mediated inhibition of hypocotyl growth in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Tavridou E, Pireyre M, Ulm R
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIF4 and PIF5 are known negative regulators of photomorphogenesis that mediate hypocotyl elongation
evidence.claim: PIF5 acts redundantly with PIF4 to activate cell elongation genes

edges.mechanism: Unphosphorylated BZR1 promotes hypocotyl cell elongation by activating cell-wall loosening genes.

evidence.evidence_sentence: bes1-D dominant mutant shows BR-hypersensitive phenotype with increased elongation
evidence.claim: BZR1 transcriptionally activates cell elongation genes (PRE, SAUR), and represses BR biosynthesis genes (feedback)

edges.mechanism: ARF6/7/8 activate SAUR19, PRE1, expansins; auxin signaling drives cell elongation.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid signaling network - a paradigm of signal integration
evidence.authors: Wang W, Bai MY, Wang ZY
evidence.evidence_sentence: ARF6 target genes encode cell wall proteins involved in cell expansion
evidence.claim: ARF6 activates cell wall remodeling genes (XTHs, EXPs, PREs) cooperatively with PIF4 and BZR1 to promote elongation

evidence.title: Three Auxin Response Factors Promote Hypocotyl Elongation

evidence.evidence_sentence: ARF6 promotes hypocotyl elongation; arf6 single mutants 80-90% of WT length
evidence.claim: ARF6 directly activates SAUR19/SAUR63 and other cell elongation genes

edges.mechanism: HY5 directly represses elongation and cell-expansion genes; hy5 mutants have long hypocotyl in light.

evidence.title: Direct phosphorylation of HY5 by SPA kinases to regulate photomorphogenesis in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wang W, Paik I, Kim J, Hou X, Sung S, Huq E
evidence.evidence_sentence: HY5 is a key positive regulator that promotes photomorphogenesis; hy5 mutant showed relatively longer hypocotyl
evidence.claim: HY5 is a master bZIP transcription factor that activates hundreds of light-responsive genes promoting photomorphogenesis (cotyledon expansion, anthocyanin) while inhibiting hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: DELLA proteins repress elongation by sequestering PIF3/PIF4, BZR1, ARF6 and by direct target repression.

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA-deficient mutants are extreme dwarfs with much reduced hypocotyl lengths
evidence.claim: DELLA represses elongation by sequestering PIF/BZR/ARF; DELLA degradation by GA derepresses growth

edges.mechanism: In darkness, EIN3 stabilization shortens hypocotyl (triple response); ein3 loss restores elongation under ACC.

evidence.title: Ethylene-Induced Stabilization of EIN3 and EIL1 Is Mediated by Proteasomal Degradation of EBF1 and EBF2 That Requires EIN2 in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: An F, Zhao Q, Ji Y, Li W, Jiang Z, Yu X, Zhang C, Han Y, He W, Liu Y, Zhang S, Ecker JR, Guo H
